OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF MEDICAL AND EMPLOYMENT RECORDS; AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 60283987) FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JAMES NJOROGE'S MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO (1) ANY MEDICAL OR EMPLOYMENT RECORDS PRECEDING JANUARY 1, 2005, AND (2) PLAINTIFF'S TAX RETURNS. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO RECOVER FOR ALLEGED INJURIES, DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO REVIEW HIS MEDICAL RECORDS PERTAINING TO PRIOR SIMILAR INJURIES OR RELATED CONDITIONS, AS WELL AS PERTAINING TO TREATMENT AND RELATED CONDITIONS POST-DATING THE ALLEGED INJURIES HERE. AS WELL, TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO RECOVER ON A CLAIM FOR LOST INCOME AND LOSS OF EARNING CAPACITY, DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO REVIEW RECORDS REFLECTING HIS COMPENSATION FROM VARIOUS SOURCES PRIOR TO AS WELL AS FOLLOWING THE ALLEGED INJURIES HERE. PLAINTIFF'S PRIVACY CONCERNS MAY BE ADDRESS BY WAY OF AN APPROPRIATE STIPULATED CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER, WHICH IS NOT OTHERWISE ADDRESSED IN THE MOVING PAPERS HERE. BOTH SIDES' REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: MARK LEHOCKEY; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; NOT REPORTED.=302/JPT.

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-13-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Order To Arbitration
Off Calendar May-05-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff James Njoroge's motion to vacate October 12, 2016 order to arbitration is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The parties are no longer required to attend judicial arbitration. Counsel for plaintiff must notify the appointed arbitrator, Mr. Glickman. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion To Quash
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, The Court adopted it's tentative ruling; PLAINTIFF JAMES NJOROGE's Motion To Quash is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's motion granted with respect to (1) any medical or employment records preceding January 1, 2005, and (2) plaintiff's tax returns. To the extent plaintiff seeks to recover for alleged injuries, defendant is entitled to review his medical records pertaining to prior similar injuries or related conditions, as well as pertaining to treatment and related conditions post-dating the alleged injuries here. As well, to the extent that plaintiff seeks to recover on a claim for lost income and loss of earning capacity, defendant is entitled to review records reflecting his compensation from various sources prior to as well as following the alleged injuries here. Plaintiff's privacy concerns may be address by way of an appropriate stipulated confidentiality order, which is not otherwise addressed in the moving papers here. Both sides' request for monetary sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to prepare Order. Judge Pro tem: Mark LeHockey; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany; Not Reported.=302/JPT.
